source.title: Scalable production and application of Pichia pastoris whole cell catalysts expressing human cytochrome P450 2C9

initial_request.research_problem: Scalable production of human CYP2C9 whole-cell catalysts in P. pastoris for preparative drug metabolite synthesis, overcoming challenges of membrane protein expression, CPR co-expression, low activity/stability, and bioreactor reproducibility.
initial_request.target_object: Human CYP2C9 whole-cell biocatalyst in P. pastoris
initial_request.known_context: hCYPs usually present low efficiency and stability; bidirectional promoters offer a simple and quick solution to enable multi-gene co-expression in which the expression of each gene can be optimally tuned.
quantifiable_goals: Reproduce shake flask activities in bioreactor
Maximize product activity (P) and yield (YP/X) or specific production rate (qp)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Scalable production of human CYP2C9 whole-cell catalysts is challenging due to membrane association, need for CPR co-expression, and low efficiency/stability.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to fine-tune CYP/CPR co-expression ratios to optimize whole-cell activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bidirectional promoters can fine-tune CYP/CPR co-expression ratios to optimize whole-cell activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use a bidirectional promoter library to co-express CYP2C9 and CPR with different regulation patterns.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Bidirectional promoters offer a simple and quick solution to enable multi-gene co-expression in which the expression of each gene can be optimally tuned.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the hypothesis by constructing promoter library and high-throughput screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: hCYPs usually present low efficiency and stability
bidirectional promoters offer a simple and quick solution to enable multi-gene co-expression in which the expression of each gene can be optimally tuned

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that bidirectional promoters can tune co-expression.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which promoter combination yields the highest CYP2C9 activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A specific bidirectional promoter construct will yield high diclofenac conversion.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct a library of bidirectional promoter constructs and screen using diclofenac and fluorometric assays.

tool_or_method.name: High-throughput screening (HTS) with diclofenac and MCF assays

parameters.assay: diclofenac conversion and fluorometric MCF assay

evolution_trajectory.observation: Construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM). The same construct with inverted promoter orientation 2C9-PDF/PDC-CPR also presented good substrate conversion reaching 39%, while all other constructs showed only 20% conversion of diclofenac, or even lower.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select best clone for bioreactor cultivation, but previous bioreactor attempts failed to reproduce shake flask results.

evidence.quote_or_span: construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM)
The same construct with inverted promoter orientation 2C9-PDF/PDC-CPR, also presented good substrate conversion reaching 39%
construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM).

The same construct with inverted promoter orientation 2C9-PDF/PDC-CPR, also presented good substrate conversion reaching 39%, while all other constructs showed only 20% conversion of diclofenac, or even lower.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Previous unpublished bioreactor attempts failed to reproduce shake flask activities.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bioreactor cultivation can be optimized to match shake flask activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Investigate bioreactor reproducibility by comparing shake flask and bioreactor cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Previous unpublished studies in our lab prior to this cultivation optimization demonstrated unexpected difficulties in the reproduction of biocatalyst activities observed in shake flasks after cultivation in methanol induced 5 L bioreactor cultures.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that current bioreactor conditions can reproduce shake flask activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: Previous unpublished studies in our lab prior to this cultivation optimization demonstrated unexpected difficulties in the reproduction of biocatalyst activities observed in shake flasks after cultivation in methanol induced 5 L bioreactor cultures.
construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether current bioreactor conditions can reproduce shake flask activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bioreactor cells can show similar diclofenac oxidation activity as shake flask cells.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare conversion rates per mg DCW between shake flask and bioreactor cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The conversion rates obtained per mg of biomass dry cell weight (DCW) for catalysts generated in this study were rather similar.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: With reproducibility confirmed, optimize pH for higher activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: the conversion rates obtained per mg of biomass dry cell weight (DCW) for catalysts generated in this study were rather similar.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Optimal pH for CYP2C9/CPR activity in bioreactor.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: pH 5 will yield higher specific enzyme activity than pH 6 or 7.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform chemostat at D=0.10 h⁻¹ and test pH 5, 6, 7.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Activity tests showed that the best condition was pH=5, obtaining a specific enzyme activity up to threefold higher than in the other conditions tested (Fig. 2). The chemostat was operated at a dilution rate of D=0.10 h⁻¹.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use optimal pH 5 to design fed-batch strategies.

evidence.quote_or_span: Activity tests showed that the best condition was pH=5, obtaining a specific enzyme activity up to threefold higher than in the other conditions tested (Fig. 2).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to maximize biocatalyst activity and yield in fed-batch.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Different feeding strategies (methanol-free vs. methanol co-feeding) will affect activity and yield.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design five fed-batch strategies: three methanol-free at μ=0.05, 0.10, 0.15 h⁻¹; two with methanol co-feeding at μ=0.05 h⁻¹ (pulses or constant 3 g/L).

parameters.FB5: μ=0.05 h⁻¹, glycerol + constant methanol 3 g/L

evolution_trajectory.observation: Five fed-batch strategies were designed: FB1 (μ=0.05 h⁻¹, glycerol only), FB2 (μ=0.10 h⁻¹, glycerol only), FB3 (μ=0.15 h⁻¹, glycerol only), FB4 (μ=0.05 h⁻¹, glycerol + methanol pulses), and FB5 (μ=0.05 h⁻¹, glycerol + constant methanol 3 g/L). A primary set of methanol-free FB cultures with glycerol as sole carbon source was performed. The methanol concentration was determined by a Methanol Sensor System and controlled by a predictive-PI control strategy.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Execute fed-batch runs and compare biocatalyst activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: a primary set of methanol-free FB cultures with glycerol as sole carbon source was performed.
the methanol concentration was determined by a Methanol Sensor System (Raven Biotech Inc., Vancouver, Canada), and controlled by performing a predictive-PI control strategy

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which strategy yields highest biocatalyst activity and production rates.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Methanol-induced strategies will give higher activity than methanol-free.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare final biocatalyst activity, YP/X, and qp across FB1-5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: When further inducing the system with methanol a twofold increase in biocatalyst activity could be observed. The use of this co-substrate further induced the protein expression, significantly increasing the production rates, leading to qp improvements of up to 1.72-fold and up to 1.63-fold of YP/X.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze the effect of specific growth rate μ on qp and YP/X.

evidence.quote_or_span: when further inducing the system with methanol a twofold increase in biocatalyst activity could be observed.
the use of this co-substrate further induced the protein expression, significantly increasing the production rates.
it led to $q_{p}$ improvements of up to 1.72-fold, and up to 1.63-fold of $Y_{P/X}$

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How specific growth rate μ affects qp and YP/X.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: μ positively affects qp, showing production is growth-coupled; high μ maximizes qp in methanol-free, while low μ with methanol maximizes YP/X.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze qp and YP/X as functions of μ and methanol presence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: It has been demonstrated that the μ has a positive effect on qp, demonstrating that the production is coupled with the cell growth. If the objective is to maximise the YP/X and, therefore, the biocatalyst activity, a bioprocess using mixed substrates based on the co-feeding of glycerol and methanol should be recommended.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Formulate design recommendations for maximizing qp or YP/X.

evidence.quote_or_span: It has been demonstrated that the $\mu$ has a positive effect on $q_{p}$ , demonstrating that the production is coupled with the cell growth.
if the objective is to maximise the $Y_{P/X}$ and, therefore, the biocatalyst activity, a bioprocess using mixed substrates based on the co-feeding of glycerol and methanol should be recommended.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to design process for different objectives (max qp vs max YP/X).
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: To maximize product activity and yield, prolong culture at low μ with methanol co-feeding; to maximize qp, use high μ glycerol-only fed-batch.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Provide design recommendations based on μ and methanol effects.

parameters.max_YP/X_strategy: low μ (0.05 h⁻¹), glycerol + methanol pulses

evolution_trajectory.observation: To maximise the product activity (P) and yield (YP/X), the process should be prolonged in time, growing the cells not only at a low μ, but also adding methanol as co-substrate, preferably through pulses. Avoiding the use of methanol would allow to avoid the important methanol-associated operational drawbacks.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply design recommendations to produce cells for preparative biotransformation.

evidence.quote_or_span: To maximise the product activity (P) and yield $(Y_{P/X})$ , the process should be prolonged in time, growing the cells not only at a low $\mu$ , but also adding methanol as co-substrate, preferably through pulses.
avoiding the use of methanol would allow to avoid the important methanol-associated operational drawbacks.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether bioreactor-produced cells can perform preparative biotransformation of ibuprofen.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bioreactor-produced cells will achieve significant conversion of ibuprofen with high selectivity for 3-OH-ibuprofen.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform preparative biotransformation of 2 mM ibuprofen in 500 mL using bioreactor-produced cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Preparative biotransformation of 2 mM ibuprofen using bioreactor-produced cells achieved 40% total conversion in 86 h, with the main metabolite 3-OH-ibuprofen accounting for 35% of the product mixture as identified by NMR spectroscopy. Conversion was performed in a baffled 2 L shake flask over a period of 86 h.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Benchmark performance against literature fission yeast system.

evidence.quote_or_span: metabolite 2 (35%), which was identified as 3-OH-ibuprofen by NMR spectroscopy
Conversion was performed in a baffled 2 L shake flask over a period of 86 h
The qualitative behaviour of both screening methods was rather similar, construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM).
2 mL of cells were mixed with diclofenac to reach a 2 mM substrate concentration and reactions were carried at 28 °C and 200 rpm
construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM).
construct 2C9-PDC/PDF-CPR showed the best conversion (53%) in the diclofenac-based assay (2 mM)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How P. pastoris system compares to fission yeast system.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: P. pastoris whole cell biocatalyst outperforms fission yeast system.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare space-time yield and specific production rate with literature values.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Values obtained with the P. pastoris whole cell biocatalyst were significantly higher—up to 55% and 21%, respectively—than the reported in the literature, in which the same proteins were expressed in a fission yeast system.

evidence.quote_or_span: values obtained with the P. pastoris whole cell biocatalyst were significantly higher—up to 55% and 21%, respectively—than the reported in the literature, in which the same proteins were expressed in a fission yeast system

evolution_trajectory.state_before: P. pastoris system outperforms fission yeast.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Biotransformation could be further improved by addressing dissolved oxygen, NADPH regeneration, and heme group generation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The biocatalytic reaction could be further improved by addressing the impact of some important parameters such as dissolved oxygen availability, efficiency of NADPH regeneration system and/or heme group generation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the biocatalytic reaction could be further improved by addressing the impact of some important parameters such as dissolved oxygen availability, efficiency of NADPH regeneration system and/or heme group generation.

validation_methods: Comparison with literature fission yeast system
success_verification.final_conclusion: A scalable P. pastoris whole-cell catalyst for CYP2C9 was developed using bidirectional promoter tuning, optimized bioreactor cultivation at pH 5 with methanol co-feeding, achieving 40% ibuprofen conversion with high selectivity and outperforming fission yeast by 55% higher STY and 21% higher specific production rate.
